# FEM Capability Matrix

This document is generated from
`finite_element_options.contracts.capability_matrix`. It is the
machine-backed source for public README capability claims and keeps
maturity separate from mere code existence.

## Maturity vocabulary

| Status | Meaning |
|---|---|
| `scaffold` | Shape exists, but no advertised user-facing route. |
| `experimental` | Optional or research route; do not treat as validated. |
| `implemented` | Code exists with focused tests, but validation is incomplete. |
| `validated` | Reference, regression, residual, or benchmark evidence exists. |
| `production-qualified` | Release/contract evidence is suitable for consumers. |
| `deprecated` | Compatibility only; replacement and removal policy are published. |
| `unsupported` | Requests fail closed before expensive numerical work. |

## Registry

| ID | Capability | Status | Workstream | Evidence / benchmark | Optional profile / absence | Scope | Limitations |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `FEM-BS-1D-EUROPEAN` | 1D European Black-Scholes FEM route. Line-mesh Black-Scholes call/put examples with explicit volatility versus variance APIs and analytical-oracle parity checks. | validated | Numerics | tests/test_black_scholes_1d.py<br>tests/test_benchmark_black_scholes.py<br>benchmark:pytest-benchmark:black_scholes_benchmark<br>reference:EuropeanOptionBs analytical oracle | base | Black-Scholes, one spatial dimension, European exercise, line-uniform scikit-fem route. | Does not validate American exercise, Heston calibration, or multi-dimensional production claims. |
| `FEM-THETA-TIME-GRID` | Theta-family time integration. Finite increasing grids, nonuniform local steps, endpoint-aware boundary/source timing, and Rannacher startup diagnostics. | validated | Numerics | tests/test_time_stepper.py<br>tests/test_state_time_coefficients.py<br>reference:FR-FEM-006 | base | Theta stepping mechanics and coefficient refresh behavior. | Adaptive time stepping, large-scale PETSc variational inequalities, and financial-product validation remain separate capabilities. |
| `FEM-AMERICAN-LCP-REFERENCE` | Reference American lower-obstacle LCP solve. Projected-SOR lower-obstacle LCP stepping for American exercise, with primal, dual, complementarity, exercise-set, nonconvergence, and Rannacher schedule diagnostics. | validated | Numerics | tests/test_american_lcp.py<br>reference:FEM-AMERICAN-LCP<br>reference:issue-41 | base | Discrete sparse lower-obstacle LCP systems coupled into the theta stepper; verifies complementarity residuals and explicit failure. | This is the base SciPy reference solver, not a PETSc/SNES VI route or a full American-product benchmark suite against QuantLib. |

## Generated-doc contract

Run this check before changing README claims or capability records:

```bash
python scripts/generate_capability_docs.py --check
```

Run this update command after intentional registry changes:

```bash
python scripts/generate_capability_docs.py --write
```
